items.briefing:
A cybercriminal group known as Silent Ransom is actively targeting law firms with extortion attacks, which is relevant to any Australian legal or professional services organisations with US connections or similar profiles. Separately, a flaw in Check Point VPN software has been actively exploited since early May — if your organisation uses Check Point VPN, check with your IT team that patches have been applied. Microsoft systems were also reportedly compromised to deliver malware to users of AI tools Claude and Gemini, so be cautious about unexpected prompts or file downloads while using these platforms.

**Practical tip:** If you use AI chat tools like Claude or Gemini for work, avoid downloading files or clicking links generated unexpectedly — report anything unusual to your IT team immediately.

featured.title: Check Point VPN Flaw Exploited Since Early May
featured.quote: Critical VPN zero-day actively exploited by ransomware gang poses immediate threat to Australian businesses.
